name: A curated list of delightful Embedded Systems libraries, RTOSes, modules, references and more!
description: A curated list of delightful Embedded Systems libraries, RTOSes, modules, references and more!

links.title: lwIP
links.description: lwIP is a small independent implementation of the TCP/IP protocol suite that has been initially developed by Adam Dunkels. Supports DHCP with AutoIP fallback, UDP, TCP, and an interrupt driven model.

links.title: uIP
links.description: A very tiny IP stack, but also a very minimal IP stack. (Not maintained anymore)

links.title: cantcoap
links.description: Small and lightweight coap encoder and decoder. Protocol level stuff is left to the user.

links.title: libcoap
links.description: libcoap implements a lightweight application-protocol for devices that are constrained their resources such as computing power, RF range, memory, bandwith, or network packet sizes

links.title: microcoap
links.description: A small CoAP implementation for microcontrollers

links.title: libnyoci
links.description: A flexible CoAP stack for embedded devices and computers. RFC7252 compatible.

links.title: CanFestival
links.description: CanFestival focuses on providing an ANSI-C platform independent CANopen® stack that can be built as master or slave nodes on PCs, Real-time IPCs, and Microcontrollers.

links.title: CANopenNode
links.description: CANopenNode is written in ANSI C in object-oriented way. It runs on different microcontrollers, as standalone application or with RTOS.

links.title: Lely CANopen Library
links.description: A CANopen implementation for both masters and slaves written in C, but with a C++ interface.

links.title: nanopb
links.description: Nanopb is a plain-C implementation of Google's Protocol Buffers data format. It is targeted at 32 bit microcontrollers, but is also fit for other embedded systems with tight (2-10 kB ROM, <1 kB RAM) memory constraints.

links.title: mpack
links.description: A C encoder/decoder for [MessagePack](http://msgpack.com) messages suitable for resource constriants embedded systems. Supports disabling dynamic memory allocation and overriding malloc, free, and realloc.

links.title: tinycbor
links.description: Intel's implementation of [CBOR](http://cbor.io/) designed for their IOT-based applications and processors

links.title: bitproto
links.description: A fast, lightweight and easy-to-use bit level data interchange format for serializing data structure. Its syntax is similar to protobuf, but very suitable for the embedded systems.

links.title: json-build
links.description: A tiny, zero-allocation JSON serializer written in ANSI C. A good fit for development in memory constrained systems.

links.title: TinyOS
links.description: A operating system designed for low-power wireless devices, such as those used in sensor networks, ubiquitous computing, personal area networks, smart buildings, and smart meters.

links.title: ContikiOS
links.description: A free Operating System with focus to provide standardized low-power wireless communication for a wide range of hardware platforms.

links.title: FreeRTOS
links.description: A free use Real Time Operating system which is widely used and supports a large number of platforms.

links.title: RIOT
links.description: A free OS for IoT devices providing foundational trust services. The trust services include device identity, sealing, attestation, and data integrity. The term “Robust” is used because the minimal trusted computing base is tiny, and because RIoT capabilities can remotely re-establish trust in devices that have been compromised by malware.

links.title: RTEMS
links.description: Real-Time Executive for Multiprocessor Systems or RTEMS is an open source Real Time Operating System (RTOS) that supports open standard application programming interfaces (API) such as POSIX. It is used in space flight, medical, networking and many more embedded devices using processor architectures including ARM, PowerPC, Intel, Blackfin, MIPS, Microblaze and more.

links.title: SafeRTOS
links.description: Certified version of FreeRTOS by TUEV SUED against IEC 61508 (basic functional safety standard) up to SIL3 (the highest safety integrity level for a single software component), ISO 26262 ASIL D (automotive standard) and EN62304 (medical device standard).

links.title: INTEGRITY/INTEGRITY-178
links.description: Two commercial RTOS variants targeting to power embedded systems with total reliability, absolute security, and maximum real-time performance. The variant INTEGRYTY-178 has a lot of safety and security certifications.

links.title: PikeOS
links.description: A commercial micro-kernel based operating system with a small footprint and certified for DO-178 (avionics), IEC-61508 (industrial), ISO-26262 (automotive).

links.title: Rocket
links.description: A free embedded operating system specifically designed to quickly and easily build small, intelligent devices in Wind Rivers cloud-based development environment, Wind River Helix™ App Cloud.

links.title: Nucleus RTOS
links.description: Commercial, highly scalable micro-kernel based real-time operating system designed for scalability and reliability.

links.title: uC/os
links.description: µC/OS-II and µC/OS-III are preemptive, highly portable, and scalable real-time kernels. You can test them out for free, but you must pay to put them into a product.

links.title: CppUTest
links.description: unit testing and mocking framework for C/C++ with a focus on working for embedded systems!

links.title: ceedling, unity and cmock
links.description: Unit testing, mocking and build framework written in C with ruby for generating and building.

links.title: doctest
links.description: A lightweight and feature-rich C++98/C++11 single-header testing framework for unit tests and TDD.

links.title: Mockitopp
links.description: A C++ mocking framework inspired by the ideas developed for Mockito written by Szczepan Faber, et al. The purpose is to provide similar construction semantics for creating mock objects leading to smaller, more readable test cases. It is designed to be a lightweight framework allowing you to mock dependencies for a system under test using a simple descriptive domain specific language. The goal is to help create simpler, less brittle test cases; ultimately, leading to less maintenance overhead in the future.

links.title: Trompeloeil
links.description: A thread safe header only mocking framework for C++14 using the Boost Software License 1.0

links.title: Catch
links.description: A modern, C++-native, header-only, test framework for unit-tests, TDD and BDD - using C++11, C++14, C++17 and later (or C++03 on the Catch1.x branch)

links.title: AREG SDK
links.description: An interface-centric real-time asynchronous communication engine for embedded and high-end applications to enable distributed- and [mist-computing](https://csrc.nist.gov/publications/detail/sp/500-325/final), where connected Things interact and provide services, as if they act like thin distributed servers.

links.title: ARM mbed
links.description: The ARM mbed IoT Device Platform provides the operating system, cloud services, tools and developer ecosystem to make the creation and deployment of commercial, standards-based IoT solutions possible at scale.

links.title: minilibd
links.description: "This collection has libraries and tools to compile D language programs for embedded or "bare metal" systems with the

links.title: DConf 2014 Talk "Tiny, Ubiquitous Machines Powered by D"
links.description: Presentation about D in tiny embedded systems.

links.title: rust-embedded
links.description: RFCs proposed by the Rust community for better embedded systems support.

links.title: embed.rs
links.description: Articles about doing embedded on rust

links.title: Nerves
links.description: Uses buildroot to deploy Elixir programs running on the Erlang VM to embedded linux boards

links.title: AdaCore
links.description: Open source maintainer of Ada with a focus on embedded use.

links.title: Tessel
links.description: A openWRT based embedded system which can run Javascript.

links.title: JerryScript
links.description: A JavaScript engine intended to run on a very constrained devices such as microcontrollers. Designed for the Internet of Things.

links.title: Nim
links.description: A statically-typed programming language inspired by Python, Ada and Modula that compiles to C, C++, and Javascript.

links.title: Ferrett
links.description: Ferret is a free software Clojure implementation for real time embedded control systems.

links.title: GERT
links.description: GERT is a modified version of Go that runs bare-metal on armv7a SOCs. The minimal set of OS primitives that Go relies on have been re-implemented entirely in Go and Plan 9 assembly inside the modified runtime. The goal of this project is to bring the benefits of a high-level, type-safe, and garbage-collected language to bare-metal embedded environments.

links.title: ESTL
links.description: C++ STL-like library for embedded developers with C++ 98 features only to maximize target platform compatibility.

links.title: ETL
links.description: Embedded Template Library which offers compile time defined container sizes, full C++ 03 and partial C++ 11 compatibility, deterministic behaviour, additional components for the embedded domain, etc.

links.title: uSTL
links.description: Partial, code size implementation of the STL.

links.title: EASTL
links.description: Electronic Arts STL - aimed for console devices. Good descripton avaliable [HERE](http://www.open-std.org/jtc1/sc22/wg21/docs/papers/2007/n2271.html)

links.title: Single File Libs
links.description: Single File libraries for C++ and C. Some might be useful for embedded systems use.

links.title: Embedded
links.description: Elicia White and Christopher White interview makes, hackers and engineers who work on embedded systems

links.title: Two's Complement Podcast
links.description: Discussions between Matt Godbolt and Ben Rady, revolving around testing, best practices, etc in C and C++ development.

links.title: Interrupt
links.description: Great in-depth blog posts from MemFault. Mostly around ARM Cortex-M series processors.

links.title: Embedsys Weekly
links.description: A weekly selection of embedded software and hardware articles.

links.title: EEVBlog
links.description: More on the electronics side, but David L. Jones tears apart embedded systems and electronic devices.

links.title: The Signal Path
links.description: Electronics equipment teardowns

links.title: Making Embedded Systems by Elicia White
links.description: Covers the basics of embedded systems architecture, design and patterns.

links.title: Test Driven Developmet for Embedded C by James Grenning
links.description: How to approach test driven development for embedded devices written in C.

links.title: uC/OS-III, The Real-Time Kernel by Jean J Labrosse
links.description: A book about the Micrium's uC/os III. It is a great resource on how RTOSes for embedded systems should work.

links.title: An Embedded Software Primer by David E. Simon
links.description: An introduction to embedded systems, with a good explanation to Real-time operating systems(RTOS).

links.title: Embedded C Coding Standards
links.description: Coding standards for embedded C from the Barr Group.

links.title: SEI CERT C Coding Standard
links.description: The SEI CERT C Coding Standard is a software coding standard for the C programming language to improve the safety, reliability, and security of software systems.

links.title: PlatformIO
links.description: PlatformIO is an open source ecosystem for IoT development

links.title: Energia
links.description: Energia is an open-source electronics prototyping platform, it brings the Wiring and Arduino framework to the Texas Instruments MSP430 based LaunchPad, it includes an IDE that is based on Processing.

links.title: XOD
links.description: An open source visual programming platform for Arduino-compatible boards.

links.title: yocto
links.description: Yocto is a tool for creating custom embedded linux systems

links.title: Buildroot
links.description: Buildroot is a simple, efficient and easy-to-use tool to generate embedded Linux systems through cross-compilation.

links.title: Mender
links.description: Open source over-the-air (OTA) software updater for embedded Linux devices.

links.title: SWUpdate
links.description: Linux Update agent with the goal to provide an efficient and safe way to update (local, remote, multiple update strategies) an embedded system.

links.title: C++ programming in Qt FrameWork Part I (udemy)
links.description: not free

links.title: C++ programming in Qt Framework: Part II (udemy)
links.description: not free

Cybersecurity Fundamentals - Construction of Secure Systems (coursera)
FPGA Turbo Series - Implementing a UART
Fun & Easy Embedded Microcontroller Communication Protocols (Udemy)
links.description: Content: I2C/TWI, SPI, UART, MODBUS RTU, CAN, USB, ETHERNET, PCIE

links.title: Introduction to Qt: A C++ Cross Platform Application Framework (pluralsight)
links.description: not free

links.title: Integrating Qt Quick with C++ (pluralsight)
links.description: not free

links.title: Learn FreeRTOS from scratch
links.description: not free - Learn about FreeRTOS basics. Practise it's APIs with different examples.

links.title: Linux OS in Embedded Systems & Linux Kernel Internals(1/2) (Udemy)
links.description: not free

links.title: Linux OS in Embedded Systems & Linux Kernel Internals(2/2) (Udemy)
links.description: not free

links.title: Introduction to Embedded Systems using 8051 Microcontroller (Udemy)
Mastering Microcontroller with Peripheral Driver Development (Udemy)
Embedded Systems Programming on ARM Cortex-M3/M4 Processor (Udemy)
Hands-on Embedded Systems, ARM Cortex Tutorials - ADCs (Udemy)
System Validation (1): Automata and behavioural equivalences (coursera)
QT C++ GUI Tutorial For Beginners (udemy)
links.description: not free

links.title: How To Implement Your First VHDL Design on FPGA (udemy)
Learn VHDL Design for use in FPGA and ASIC Digital Systems (udemy)
Learn VHDL, ISE and FPGA by Designing a basic Home Alarm (udemy)
The Embedded Pre-Interview! (udemy)
links.description: not free - Evaluate your Embedded Systems knowledge, in just 100 minutes, for any entry level job
